incidents.victim: Dutch hosting and internet service providers
incidents.vector: Chinese cyber-espionage campaign, publicly linked by allies to three Chinese technology firms working for Beijing, compromised routers at smaller Dutch hosting and ISP providers throughout 2025, per the Dutch military intelligence service (MIVD).
incidents.impact: MIVD assesses only a limited proportion of Chinese operations against Dutch interests are detected; attackers accessed routers at smaller providers but were not observed moving deeper into internal networks. PLA cyber reorganization is credited for the uptick.

incidents.vector: North Korean IT-worker operators use known actor infrastructure and AI-generated personas to query Workday Recruiting APIs, submit tailored job applications, and then configure payroll records after hire to draw wages from unwitting employers.
incidents.impact: Microsoft Threat Intelligence reports repeated anomalous API access patterns to /hrrecruiting endpoints, fraudulent applications that pass screening, and impossible-travel alerts on newly hired accounts in the first two months on the job — creating insider access risk and revenue for DPRK.

incidents.vector: Attacker used OAuth tokens stolen from a Context.ai employee infected with Lumma Stealer (via Roblox cheat malware) to hijack a Vercel employee's Google Workspace account and reach Vercel environments.
incidents.impact: Vercel environments, environment variables, and a limited subset of customer credentials were accessed; affected customers were told to rotate credentials. A party claiming to be ShinyHunters demanded a $2 million ransom and offered stolen data for sale; Mandiant and law enforcement are engaged.

incidents.vector: Attackers impersonated an authorized staff account in late 2025 to access a service connected to the ÉduConnect student account management platform.
incidents.impact: Personal data on an undisclosed number of students was leaked. The ministry disclosed the incident in mid-April 2026 as part of a wider wave of breaches affecting French public institutions.

incidents.victim: Israeli water treatment and desalination operators (attempted)
incidents.vector: Custom OT malware (ZionSiphon) designed to run only on Israeli IP ranges and to locate hardcoded paths for OT software used by named Israeli water and desalination companies; once matched, it would adjust pump pressure and chlorine levels to maximum.
incidents.impact: Darktrace's analysis found the sabotage logic did not trigger in the wild due to an XOR encoding bug in the IP geolocation check, causing the malware to self-delete. Unclear whether the sample was deployed operationally or was pre-release; political strings reference support for Iran, Palestine, and Yemen.

incidents.vector: Attackers spent days pre-positioning fake token pools on Rhea Finance and then exploited vulnerabilities in the platform's margin trading feature to route trades through the fake pools.
incidents.impact: Approximately $18.4 million in crypto assets drained from the Rhea Finance DeFi protocol.

incidents.vector: Exploitation of a vulnerability in Kelp DAO's rsETH bridge built on LayerZero allowed attackers to withdraw restaked Ether tokens.
incidents.impact: Roughly $292-300 million in crypto was stolen — the largest single crypto heist of 2026 so far. About $175 million has been moved into laundering channels via THORChain, Umbra, and BitTorrent as parts of the industry try to block the funds.

incidents.vector: Cyberattack on the city's network prompted an emergency shutdown; neighboring Leon County disconnected its network to contain spread, language consistent with ransomware response.
incidents.impact: City IT systems taken offline, multiple public websites and services unavailable, and Leon County severed its connection to the city network as a precaution. Attack type and threat actor not yet disclosed.

incidents.vector: Sustained, sophisticated Distributed Denial-of-Service (DDoS) attack against Bluesky infrastructure, with follow-on DDoS attempts throughout the week.
incidents.impact: Bluesky operated intermittently for nearly a day on April 15-16, with users experiencing prolonged outages. Follow-on DDoS attempts the same week were mitigated without another full outage.

incidents.victim: Managed service providers and downstream customers (incl. a dental software firm)
incidents.vector: Unauthenticated remote code execution in BeyondTrust (Bomgar) Remote Support appliances (CVE-2026-1731) used to take over upstream RMM servers and pivot into downstream customer networks.
incidents.impact: One April 15 intrusion at an MSP forced isolation of 78 businesses and exploitation of four downstream customers; at least one incident deployed LockBit 3.0 ransomware. Other compromises pushed AnyDesk/Atera for persistence and added rogue admin accounts.

incidents.victim: French National Agency for Secure Documents (ANTS)
incidents.vector: A security incident on the ants.gouv.fr portal, which handles passport, ID card, residence permit, and driver's license applications, was detected on April 15 and is still under investigation.
incidents.impact: Potential exposure of login credentials, names, email addresses, dates of birth, unique account identifiers, and in some cases postal addresses, places of birth, and phone numbers of individual and professional users. Uploaded application attachments are reported as unaffected.

incidents.victim: Government agencies and foreign-ministry/law-enforcement organizations (200+ orgs, 18,000+ SOHO routers)
incidents.vector: Exploitation of known flaws in older, mostly end-of-life Mikrotik and TP-Link SOHO routers to modify DNS settings and redirect victim traffic through attacker-controlled servers, enabling theft of Microsoft Office OAuth tokens without deploying malware.
incidents.impact: Stealthy, malware-free harvesting of Microsoft 365 authentication tokens from more than 18,000 networks at peak (Dec 2025), with at least 200 organizations — primarily foreign ministries, law enforcement, and email providers — caught in the campaign. FBI, NSA, and NCSC issued advisories and the FBI has moved to dismantle key infrastructure.

incidents.victim: LiteLLM, Checkmarx, Trivy, Telnyx PyPI users (developer ecosystem)
incidents.vector: TeamPCP used likely stolen developer credentials to obtain write access to trusted repositories, then injected credential-harvesting payloads into LiteLLM (~97M monthly downloads), poisoned two Checkmarx GitHub Actions workflows, and pivoted from an earlier Trivy compromise.
incidents.impact: Malware silently harvested cloud credentials, access keys, and AI API keys from installs across the developer ecosystem; 300+ GB of data reportedly held for extortion. Campaign has expanded (SANS ISC tracking ongoing updates) and is linked in reporting to possible Lapsus$ collaboration.

incidents.vector: Crimson Collective obtained a GitHub Personal Access Token accidentally published on a public website, used TruffleHog to scan the victim's GitHub repositories for more secrets, and then abused Microsoft Graph API calls to explore and exfiltrate data from Azure cloud storage.
incidents.impact: Data exfiltration from Azure cloud storage and attempted injection of secret-harvesting code into multiple GitHub repositories (largely foiled by expired secrets). Cisco Talos's first documented Crimson Collective engagement demonstrates the group's shift to living-off-legitimate-cloud APIs.
